study_captions.A: Aerial survey of Felicity Island (2012). Diverse colony of 1397 birds across 13 species, dominated by Laughing Gull (57% of total). Top species: Laughing Gull (797), Royal Tern (306), Sandwich Tern (119) and 10 others. Primary habitat: vegetation. Automated annotation recovery: 716 of 1397 birds (51%).
study_captions.B: Aerial survey of Gaillard Island (2011). Diverse colony of 93 birds across 7 species, dominated by Brown Pelican (53% of total). Top species: Brown Pelican (49), Laughing Gull (16), Canada Goose (14) and 4 others. Primary habitat: vegetation. Automated annotation recovery: 64 of 93 birds (69%).
study_captions.C: Aerial survey of North Deer Island (2010). Diverse colony of 115 birds across 11 species, dominated by White Ibis (30% of total). Top species: White Ibis (35), Neotropic Cormorant (20), Laughing Gull (17) and 8 others. Primary habitat: bare ground. Automated annotation recovery: 55 of 115 birds (48%).
study_captions.D: Aerial survey of Raccoon Island (2011). Diverse colony of 538 birds across 7 species, dominated by Brown Pelican (53% of total). Top species: Brown Pelican (285), Laughing Gull (244), Great Egret (4) and 4 others. Primary habitat: marsh grass. Automated annotation recovery: 336 of 538 birds (62%).
